mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single quarter, it signals a labor market deterioration that triggers recession fears, compressing equity multiples and driving negative forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE exceeds 4.5% in a monthly reading
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of consumer credit (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicates a synchronized demand shock that erodes corporate revenue growth expectations, leading to negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not systematically decline when both series show simultaneous month-over-month declines, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL > 3.5% YoY), a decline in housing starts (HOUST) acts as a leading indicator of cooling aggregate demand, which historically precedes positive equity returns as inflationary pressures ease.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain negative or flat when housing starts decline alongside high inflation, or the conditional IC fails to exceed 0.05 in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects robust economic activity that supports corporate earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of observations, or the rolling Rank IC falls below 0.01 over a 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP growth rate (GDPC1) falling below 1.5% annualized signals a deceleration in economic momentum that triggers risk-off positioning, driving negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Equity returns do not decline following GDP deceleration below the threshold, or the signal's hit rate drops below 40% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) and stagnant employment growth (PAYEMS) indicates a policy-induced tightening that suppresses valuation multiples and earnings growth, producing negative forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not systematically fall when both rates rise and payroll growth stagnates, or the interaction effect vanishes in a rolling 3-year regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-unemployment environment (UNRATE < 4.0%), a sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects capacity expansion that supports corporate profitability, driving positive equity returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth fails to correlate with positive 5-day returns when unemployment is below 4%, or the conditional predictive power drops below statistical significance.
